they take movies you purchased digitally away from you again as soon as it's not financially beneficial for them anymore to let you keep them.

paragraphs.text: What bothers me the most is that companies, having the interest of earning the maximum amount of money possible, can control for how long we can access things they let us believe we own. There are countless instances where transactions take place but it's only definite in one direction. They keep my money forever. I usually don't get to keep the product i obtained through the transaction forever. And it's not even a software as a service, where recurrent cost makes sense. It's for the likes of Music, Movies, Games and all kinds of Media. It feels impossible to buy music digitally and get an audio-file to download, which would be analog to receiving a CD. Instead i have to subscribe to a service, to download Media, which still ist DRM protected on my machine. So i only have the freedom in listening to what i purchased through what the company allows me to, or what they maintain. It's pretty similar to movies, where companies are free to take movies you purchased digitally away from you again as soon as it's not financially beneficial for them anymore to let you keep them. I also would love to see this Fairness Act to target Game Studios as well, since they develop and target their games in a way, where the consumer has no way of retaining what they paid for. The biggest example is "the crew". From a technical and developer standpoint it would've been more than feasible developing the game such that a server could be run privately, such that you were able to play with your friends in a local setting. Instead it was designed to be taken away, as are a terrifying amount of games nowadays. I hope the world of Digital transactions and Media will turn into a less dystopian and more enjoyable and fair experience. Thank you for reading this and thank you a lot for your Work and i hope the Digital Fairness Act will reestablish a fair relationship between consumers and companies.
